Meaning.Vi: Bong bóng (khí bao quanh bởi chất lỏng)
Meaning.En: A sphere of gas or vapor enclosed in liquid or solid, especially one formed by boiling or effervescence.
Meaning.Example: She watched the bubbles rise to the surface of the boiling water.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả hiện tượng khí tạo thành bong bóng trong chất lỏng khi đun sôi hoặc sủi bọt.
Situation.En: Describing gas forming bubbles in a liquid during boiling or effervescence.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả bong bóng xà phòng hoặc bong bóng không khí trong nước.
Situation.En: Describing soap bubbles or air bubbles in water.

Meaning.En: A rapid increase and subsequent collapse in the price of an asset, driven by speculation and exuberance.
Meaning.Example: The housing bubble burst in 2008, triggering a global financial crisis.

Situation.Vi: Sử dụng khi nói về sự bùng nổ và vỡ bong bóng giá tài sản trong kinh tế.
Situation.En: Used when discussing asset price booms and crashes in economics.

Meaning.Vi: Bong bóng (ẩn dụ: thế giới tách biệt, ảo tưởng)
Meaning.En: A state of isolation or separation from reality, often characterized by unrealistic optimism or detachment.
Meaning.Example: He lives in a bubble of privilege and doesn’t understand everyday struggles.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả một người sống trong thế giới ảo tưởng, tách biệt khỏi thực tế.
Situation.En: Describing someone living in an unrealistic, isolated world detached from reality.

Meaning.En: To form or cause to form small spherical pockets of gas in a liquid or other substance.
Meaning.Example: The soda began to bubble as soon as the bottle was opened.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả chất lỏng sủi bọt do khí thoát ra.
Situation.En: Describing a liquid foaming or fizzing due to gas escaping.

Meaning.En: To express strong emotion suddenly and intensely.
Meaning.Example: Her excitement bubbled over when she heard the good news.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả cảm xúc mãnh liệt tràn ra (vui, tức giận, v.v.).
Situation.En: Describing intense emotions suddenly erupting (joy, anger, etc.).
